title: Dakshina Kannada records 54% rainfall deficit as weak monsoon dries rivers - newskarnataka.com
article_text: “This year’s monsoon has clearly weakened. If rainfall does not improve before the end of June, paddy farmers are likely to face difficulties. Paddy nursery activities are currently under way, and if adequate rainfall is not received before transplantation begins in July, cultivation will be affected. Alternative measures will also be considered,” said Honnappa Gowda, Joint Director of the Agriculture Department in Mangaluru.

title: Meteorological Department issues yellow alert in 6 districts as heavy rain lashes Keralam - News On AIR
article_text:
In Keralam, Meteorological Department has issued yellow alert, indicating isolated heavy rain in six of the 14 districts.

Fishermen have been advised not to venture into the sea along the Kerala and Lakshadweep coasts for fishing.

Winds with speeds of up to 60 km per hour are also predicted along the Kerala coast today. Heavy rain was reported from isolated places in Kerala on Thursday.
